A_DQ_experience.theme: Direct Querying felt mechanical and repetitive
A_DQ_experience.quote: It felt very mechanical — just answering the same type of question over and over.

B_UPF_experience.theme: UPF was cognitively demanding but felt intelligent
B_UPF_experience.quote: A bit challenging at first because I'd never articulated my rules before.

C_PAR_experience.theme: PAR felt proactive and led to a sense of being understood
C_PAR_experience.quote: Nice that it did the work of inferring rather than just asking.

D_overall_preference.theme: Overall preferred PAR for real deployment
D_overall_preference.quote: For a real robot, I think PAR would work best in my home.

E_individual_notes.quote: I don't really have set rules, so having the robot guess and confirm was easier.

A_DQ_experience.theme: Direct Querying felt mechanical and repetitive
A_DQ_experience.quote: It was straightforward but a bit tedious after a while.

B_UPF_experience.theme: UPF was cognitively demanding but felt intelligent
B_UPF_experience.quote: I liked being asked about my principles first — it felt more like a real conversation.

C_PAR_experience.theme: PAR felt proactive and led to a sense of being understood
C_PAR_experience.quote: I loved that it made suggestions — felt like it was paying attention.

D_overall_preference.theme: Overall preferred PAR for real deployment
D_overall_preference.quote: If I had to choose for everyday use, PAR wins — less repetition, more understanding.

E_individual_notes.quote: I don't really have set rules, so having the robot guess and confirm was easier.
